title: Covellia conglomerata
taxonomy: Covellia conglomerata is a species of lichenized fungus in the family Lichinaceae. It belongs to the genus Covellia, which is a group of lichens that are often found growing on tree bark. The classification of Covellia conglomerata is as follows: kingdom Fungi, phylum Ascomycota, class Lichinomycetes, order Lichinales, family Lichinaceae, genus Covellia, species C. conglomerata. This classification indicates its position within the fungal kingdom and its close relationship to other lichens in the Lichinaceae family. Further research may be necessary to confirm the accuracy of this classification.

intro: Covellia conglomerata, also known as False Borage or False Cornflower, is a flowering plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, including inflammation, respiratory problems, and digestive issues, due to its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Covellia conglomerata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Covellia conglomerata is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine in various forms, including capsules and topical creams.
